question: Spread: Kōchi United SC (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for March 29 at 1:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Kōchi United SC" if Kōchi United SC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Tokushima Vortis".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on jleague.jp. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Tokushima Vortis vs. Kōchi United SC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for March 29 at 1:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tokushima Vortis holds a clear edge as a J2 League side facing J3's Kōchi United SC in the Emperor's Cup third round, with trader consensus pricing Vortis victory around 70% implied probability due to superior squad depth and home advantage at Pocarisweat Stadium. Vortis sits mid-table in J2 after a mixed run of two wins in five, hampered by key midfielder Daiki Sato's hamstring doubt per official reports, while Kōchi boasts J3 momentum with three straight away wins but lacks firepower against higher-tier defenses. Head-to-head limited, historical cup upsets rare for J2 favorites; rest advantage to Vortis post-midweek bye could sway tight margins, though Kōchi's counterattack threat tempers full confidence.

events.description: More markets for the J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for March 29 at 1:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Reilac Shiga's strong home record drives trader consensus favoring them against Tegevajaro Miyazaki in this J3 League clash, with Shiga unbeaten in their last five home games and sitting comfortably mid-table. events.title: Kamatamare Sanuki vs. Tochigi SC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for March 29 at 1:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Tochigi SC in their J2 League clash at struggling Kamatamare Sanuki's home ground, driven by Tochigi's 4-1 edge in the last five head-to-heads and unbeaten run in their past three away games. Key absences hit Sanuki's midfield, but Tochigi arrive near full strength with a debutant forward boosting attack. Both sides' low-scoring tendencies point to under 2.5 goals as a market watchpoint in this survival scrap.

events.title: Gainare Tottori vs. Sagan Tosu - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for March 29 at 1:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sagan Tosu enters as heavy favorites against J3 side Gainare Tottori in this Emperor's Cup third-round clash, with trader consensus reflecting the J.League 1 club's superior talent and experience despite their dismal season form, sitting 19th in J1 with just four wins in 32 matches. Tottori, comfortably mid-table in J3, rides momentum from a penalty-shootout upset over J2's V-Varen Nagasaki in the prior round, but faces a step up against Tosu's attacking threats like Taichi Kikuchi. No major injuries reported for either, though Tosu's road woes (winless in nine away J1 games) and rest disadvantage after league duty could fuel upset potential; historical cup shocks in Japan add uncertainty to the implied probabilities.

events.title: Gainare Tottori vs. Sagan Tosu - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for March 29 at 1:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sagan Tosu enters as heavy favorites against J3 side Gainare Tottori in this Emperor's Cup third-round clash, with trader consensus reflecting the J.League 1 club's superior talent and experience despite their dismal season form, sitting 19th in J1 with just four wins in 32 matches. Tottori, comfortably mid-table in J3, rides momentum from a penalty-shootout upset over J2's V-Varen Nagasaki in the prior round, but faces a step up against Tosu's attacking threats like Taichi Kikuchi. No major injuries reported for either, though Tosu's road woes (winless in nine away J1 games) and rest disadvantage after league duty could fuel upset potential; historical cup shocks in Japan add uncertainty to the implied probabilities.

events.title: Iwaki FC vs. Albirex Niigata - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for March 29 at 1:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Iwaki FC's strong home form in J3 League play has fueled trader consensus favoring the underdogs against J1 side Albirex Niigata in this Emperor's Cup clash, with markets reflecting upset potential amid Niigata's middling away record and recent defensive lapses. Iwaki, leading J3 standings on a five-match unbeaten streak, benefits from Fukushima Azalea Stadium advantage and momentum from cup progression, while Niigata grapples with key midfielder Motoki Hasegawa's doubtful status per official reports and fatigue from midweek J1 duties. Historical J1-vs-J3 cup upsets underscore unpredictability, but traders weigh Niigata's superior squad depth against Iwaki's pressing intensity and rest edge as pivotal factors ahead of kickoff.

events.title: Iwaki FC vs. Júbilo Iwata - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for March 29 at 1:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Júbilo Iwata's experience in J2 League fuels trader consensus favoring them over J3 frontrunners Iwaki FC in this J.League YBC Levain Cup 2nd-round clash at Iwaki Green Stadium. Iwata advanced past J1 Sagan Tosu on penalties, showcasing resilience, while Iwaki stunned J2 Ventforet Kofu 2-1 in the opener, riding unbeaten streak across five matches and potent home scoring. No official injury reports alter lineups significantly, though Iwata's away form lags (winless in last three road games). Cup dynamics amplify upset risk for the visitors, with Iwaki's momentum and crowd support testing Iwata's quality amid J2 title chase pressures. Markets reflect ~65% implied Iwata win probability, balancing hierarchy against form surge.

Iwata advanced past J1 Sagan Tosu on penalties, showcasing resilience, while Iwaki stunned J2 Ventforet Kofu 2-1 in the opener, riding unbeaten streak across five matches and potent home scoring. No official injury reports alter lineups significantly, though Iwata's away form lags (winless in last three road games). Cup dynamics amplify upset risk for the visitors, with Iwaki's momentum and crowd support testing Iwata's quality amid J2 title chase pressures. Markets reflect ~65% implied Iwata win probability, balancing hierarchy against form surge.

eventMetadata.context_description: Renofa Yamaguchi's solid home form against Oita Trinita, unbeaten in their last five meetings at Ishin Me-Life Stadium, drives trader sentiment toward the hosts in this J.League 2 clash. Yamaguchi sit comfortably mid-table after a three-match unbeaten streak, bolstered by key midfielder Taiga Son's return from injury per official reports, while Oita languish near the relegation zone with just one win in their past eight away games. Oita's defense has conceded 2.1 goals per match on the road, amplifying Yamaguchi's scoring edge, though forecasters note Oita's desperation could spark an upset amid playoff positioning pressures. Market odds reflect crowd wisdom on these trends, with weather-neutral conditions ahead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Matsumoto Yamaga FC holds a slight edge in trader consensus for their J3 League clash at Fukushima United FC's home ground, driven by superior recent form with three wins in their last five matches compared to Fukushima's lone victory amid defensive struggles conceding 10 goals. Official injury reports confirm no major absences for Matsumoto's key midfielders, while Fukushima misses suspended defender Yuto Suzuki, weakening their backline against Matsumoto's potent attack led by forward Daizen Takahiro. Home advantage tempers Fukushima's underdog status, but Matsumoto's road record (4-2-1) and head-to-head dominance (3-1 aggregate last two meetings) underpin the implied probabilities, with rest days favoring the visitors ahead of a grueling schedule stretch.

question: Spread: Matsumoto Yamaga FC (-2.5)
description:

question: Will Oman strike Iran by March 31?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed country initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate by March 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by the listed country's military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by a missile launched by the listed country, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by ground operatives of the listed country will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by ground operatives of the listed country will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probabilities for major countries launching direct military action against Iran by March 31, driven by sustained restraint despite heightened Middle East tensions. Israel conducted limited strikes on Iranian targets in October 2024 but has since focused on proxies like Hezbollah in Lebanon and Hamas in Gaza, with no new announcements of Iran-focused operations. The US continues Houthi strikes in Yemen— Iran-backed but not mainland Iran—while emphasizing diplomacy on Tehran's nuclear program amid IAEA reports of non-compliance. Incoming Trump administration signals tougher Iran policy post-January 20, but absent concrete triggers like proxy escalations, markets price stability; watch for UN Security Council sessions or Israeli security cabinet decisions.

question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $10,000 by end of March?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of March 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket tilts toward gold futures (GC) surpassing key resistance levels by March 31, driven primarily by persistent expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts amid cooling inflation data. Spot gold trades near $2,670/oz, with COMEX futures implying a 55% probability of hitting $2,750 amid dollar weakness (DXY at 103.5) and rising real yields pressuring bullion lower short-term. Central bank purchases—over 1,000 tonnes in 2024 per World Gold Council—and geopolitical risks in Ukraine and Middle East bolster upside. Watch March 19 FOMC for cut signals and Feb 12 CPI release; a print below 2.9% YoY could propel prices toward $2,800, while hawkish Fed rhetoric risks a pullback to $2,600 support.
